model_narrative: We ship a product from several plants to several markets. Each plant-to-market route has a known shipping cost per case that grows with distance. We decide how many cases to ship on each route. The objective is to make the total shipping cost as small as possible.

sets.name: i
sets.doc: the plants that ship the product

sets.name: j
sets.doc: the markets that receive the product

params.name: f
params.doc: freight rate in dollars per case per thousand miles

params.name: a
params.doc: shipping capacity of each plant, in cases

params.name: b
params.doc: demand of each market, in cases

params.name: d
params.doc: distance from a plant to a market, in thousands of miles

params.name: c
params.doc: shipping cost per case on a plant-to-market route, in thousands of dollars; it is the freight rate times the distance

vars.name: x
vars.doc: the number of cases shipped from a plant to a market

vars.name: z
vars.doc: total transportation cost in thousands of dollars

constraints.name: z
constraints.intent: The total transportation cost is the per-case shipping cost on each route multiplied by the number of cases shipped on it, summed over every plant and market. Set the cost variable equal to that total.

constraints.name: x[i,
constraints.intent: No plant can ship out more than its capacity. For each plant, the total cases sent from it to all markets must not exceed that plant's capacity.

constraints.name: x[i,
constraints.intent: Every market's demand must be met. For each market, the total cases shipped into it from all plants must be at least that market's demand.

query: We're looking at de-risking how much San Diego leans on its Chicago run, since that lane carries a big share of the plant's outbound volume. There's a proposal on the table that the San-Diego-to-Chicago route should account for no more than 40 percent of everything San Diego ships across all markets combined. What does the optimizer return for the total cost and the new routing?
expected_pyomo: model.sd_chicago_share = Constraint(expr=model.x['san-diego', 'chicago'] <= 0.40 * sum(model.x['san-diego', j] for j in model.j))

query: It nags at me that Seattle sits idle on both interior markets while San Diego shoulders all of Chicago and Topeka. Shouldn't Seattle be carrying at least 200 cases combined into Chicago and Topeka so we aren't single-threaded on San Diego inland? What's keeping the model from leaning on Seattle for that interior volume?
